title: MSETEX
description: Atomically sets multiple string keys with a shared expiration in a single operation. Supports flexible argument parsing where condition and expiration flags can appear in any order.
syntax:
MSETEX numkeys key value [key value ...] [NX | XX] [EX seconds |
  PX milliseconds | EXAT unix-time-seconds |
  PXAT unix-time-milliseconds | KEEPTTL]
complexity: O(N) where N is the number of keys to set.

redis_py.signature: msetex(, mapping: Mapping[AnyKeyT, EncodableT],, data_persist_option: Optional[DataPersistOptions] = None,, ex: Optional[ExpiryT] = None,, px: Optional[ExpiryT] = None,, exat: Optional[AbsExpiryT] = None,, pxat: Optional[AbsExpiryT] = None,, keepttl: bool = False,)

returns.description: 1 if the all the keys were set, 0 if no key was set (at least one key already existed)

jedis.signature: boolean msetex(final MSetExParams params, final byte[]... keysvalues)

returns.description: 1 if the all the keys were set, 0 if no key was set (at least one key already existed)

jedis.signature: boolean msetex(final MSetExParams params, final String... keysvalues)

params.description: the map of keys and values.
params.name: map

params.description: the MSetExArgs specifying NX/XX and expiration.
params.name: args

returns.description: Boolean from integer-reply: 1 if all keys were set, 0 otherwise. @since 7.1

content:
Atomically sets multiple string keys with an optional shared expiration in a single operation. ## Required arguments
numkeys The number of keys being set.
key value [key value ...] A series of key/value pairs.
## Optional arguments
condition and expiration flags The `MSETEX` command supports a set of options that modify its behavior: * `NX` -- Set the keys and their expiration time only if none of the specified keys exist. * `XX` -- Set the keys and their expiration time only if all of the specified keys already exist. * `EX seconds` -- Set the specified expiration time in seconds (a positive integer). * `PX milliseconds` -- Set the specified expiration time in milliseconds (a positive integer). * `EXAT unix-time-seconds` -- Set the specified Unix time in seconds (a positive integer) at which the key(s) will expire. * `PXAT unix-time-milliseconds` -- Set the specified Unix time in milliseconds (a positive integer) at which the key(s) will expire. * `KEEPTTL` -- Retain the time to live associated with the keys.
## Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility | Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ❌ Standard
❌ Active-Active | ❌ Standard
❌ Active-Active | | ## Return information {{< multitabs id="return-info" tab1="RESP2" tab2="RESP3" >}} [Integer reply]({{< relref "/develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers" >}}): 0 if none of the keys were set; 1 if all of the keys were set. -tab-sep- [Integer reply]({{< relref "/develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers" >}}): 0 if none of the keys were set; 1 if all of the keys were set. {{< /multitabs >}}
intro: Atomically sets multiple string keys with an optional shared expiration in a single operation.
